prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_disruption_record origin_segment_1)
(assign_vehicle_to_case origin_segment_1 vehicle_unit_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(confirm_case_schedule origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(register_disruption_record destination_segment_1)
(assign_vehicle_to_case destination_segment_1 vehicle_unit_2)
(assign_time_window_to_case destination_segment_1 time_window_1)
(confirm_case_schedule destination_segment_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case destination_segment_1 time_window_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(flag_affected_zone_for_segment origin_segment_1 affected_zone_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case destination_segment_1 time_window_1)
(flag_alternate_zone_for_destination destination_segment_1 alternate_zone_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case destination_segment_1 time_window_1)
(assign_operator_team_to_case origin_segment_1 operator_team_1)
(assign_operator_and_mark_origin_segment origin_segment_1 affected_zone_1 operator_team_1)
(release_operator_team_from_case origin_segment_1 operator_team_1)
(assign_operator_team_to_case destination_segment_1 operator_team_1)
(assign_operator_and_mark_destination_segment destination_segment_1 alternate_zone_1 operator_team_1)
(release_operator_team_from_case destination_segment_1 operator_team_1)
(instantiate_contingency_plan origin_segment_1 destination_segment_1 affected_zone_1 alternate_zone_1 contingency_plan_template_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(confirm_plan_time contingency_plan_template_1 origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(assign_restoration_to_origin_segment origin_segment_1 contingency_plan_template_1)
(assign_restoration_to_destination_segment destination_segment_1 contingency_plan_template_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case origin_segment_1 time_window_1)
(attach_claim_document_to_case origin_segment_1 claim_document_1 time_window_1)
(finalize_restoration_and_release_vehicle_for_origin origin_segment_1 vehicle_unit_1 claim_document_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case destination_segment_1 time_window_1)
(attach_claim_document_to_case destination_segment_1 claim_document_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case destination_segment_1 time_window_1)
(finalize_restoration_and_release_vehicle_for_destination destination_segment_1 vehicle_unit_2 claim_document_1)
(register_disruption_record replanning_agent_1)
(assign_vehicle_to_case replanning_agent_1 vehicle_unit_3)
(assign_time_window_to_case replanning_agent_1 time_window_1)
(confirm_case_schedule replanning_agent_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case replanning_agent_1 time_window_1)
(register_disruption_record replanning_agent_2)
(assign_vehicle_to_case replanning_agent_2 vehicle_unit_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case replanning_agent_2 time_window_1)
(confirm_case_schedule replanning_agent_2 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case replanning_agent_2 time_window_1)
(assign_depot_to_plan replanning_agent_1 depot_asset_1 contingency_plan_template_1)
(assign_depot_to_plan replanning_agent_2 depot_asset_2 contingency_plan_template_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case replanning_agent_1 time_window_1)
(authorize_agent_for_plan replanning_agent_1 depot_asset_1 contingency_plan_template_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case replanning_agent_1 time_window_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case replanning_agent_2 time_window_1)
(authorize_agent_for_plan replanning_agent_2 depot_asset_2 contingency_plan_template_1 time_window_1)
(release_time_window_from_case replanning_agent_2 time_window_1)
(assign_equipment_to_agent replanning_agent_1 equipment_1)
(assign_operator_team_to_case replanning_agent_1 operator_team_1)
(assign_authority_approval_to_agent replanning_agent_1 authority_approval_1)
(equip_agent_for_plan_standard replanning_agent_1 equipment_1 operator_team_1 depot_asset_1 contingency_plan_template_1)
(process_agent_authority_approval replanning_agent_1 authority_approval_1 depot_asset_1 contingency_plan_template_1)
(release_operator_team_from_case replanning_agent_1 operator_team_1)
(release_equipment_from_agent replanning_agent_1 equipment_1)
(release_authority_approval_from_agent replanning_agent_1 authority_approval_1)
(finalize_agent_activation replanning_agent_1)
(assign_time_window_to_case replanning_agent_1 time_window_1)
(attach_claim_document_to_case replanning_agent_1 claim_document_1 time_window_1)
(finalize_restoration_and_release_vehicle_for_agent replanning_agent_1 vehicle_unit_3 claim_document_1)
(assign_equipment_to_agent replanning_agent_2 equipment_1)
(assign_operator_team_to_case replanning_agent_2 operator_team_1)
(assign_authority_approval_to_agent replanning_agent_2 authority_approval_1)
(equip_agent_for_plan_standard replanning_agent_2 equipment_1 operator_team_1 depot_asset_2 contingency_plan_template_1)
(process_agent_authority_approval replanning_agent_2 authority_approval_1 depot_asset_2 contingency_plan_template_1)
(release_operator_team_from_case replanning_agent_2 operator_team_1)
(release_equipment_from_agent replanning_agent_2 equipment_1)
(release_authority_approval_from_agent replanning_agent_2 authority_approval_1)
(finalize_agent_activation replanning_agent_2)
(assign_time_window_to_case replanning_agent_2 time_window_1)
(attach_claim_document_to_case replanning_agent_2 claim_document_1 time_window_1)
(finalize_restoration_and_release_vehicle_for_agent replanning_agent_2 vehicle_unit_1 claim_document_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many objects and actions. The goal is to make four components recover complete: origin_segment_1, destination_segment_1, replanning_agent_1, and replanning_agent_2.


## Addressing Component Recovery  
- The task focuses on restoring four components to full functionality: origin_segment_1, destination_segment_1, replanning_agent_1, and replanning_agent_2.  
- The complexity arises from managing multiple objects and actions to ensure complete recovery.

## Addressing Component Recovery  
- The process for each component (origin_segment_1, destination_segment_1, replanning_agent_1, replanning_agent_2) starts with registering a disruption since no initial disruptions or schedules exist.  
- Recovery involves sequential steps: register disruption, assign vehicle and time window, confirm schedule, manage operators and materials, flag zones, instantiate contingency plans, attach claims, and finalize restoration.  

## Examining Agent Coordination  
- Both replanning_agent_1 and replanning_agent_2 are linked to the same segments and plan, requiring coordinated actions for recovery.  
- Agents have service options assigned, but service_option_2 is assigned to replanning_agent_2 despite only service_option_1 being listed as available, suggesting potential inconsistencies.  

## Navigating Planning Actions  
- For segments, restoration scheduling requires a selected plan, validated readiness, and confirmed time, while agents use activation actions like finalize_agent_activation to set restoration schedules.  
- Final recovery for all components involves attaching claims (requiring scheduled restoration and assigned time windows) and finalizing with vehicle release, highlighting the need for a coordinated action sequence.

## Investigating Vehicle Constraints  
- The problem involves four components (origin_segment_1, destination_segment_1, replanning_agent_1, replanning_agent_2) requiring recovery, but only three vehicles are available.  
- Finalizing recovery for each component needs a vehicle assigned, as all finalize actions require component_vehicle_assigned, creating a resource bottleneck.  

## Exploring Agent Scheduling  
- Agents (replanning_agent_1, replanning_agent_2) lack initial time_window_assigned, suggesting scheduling is needed for restoration.  
- Activation actions like finalize_agent_activation set component_restoration_scheduled for agents without directly requiring a vehicle, but claims still need scheduling and vehicle assignment.  

## Considering Recovery Workflow  
- Recovery for all components follows a sequence: register disruption, schedule restoration, attach claims, and finalize with vehicle release.  
- Vehicles cannot be reused until finalization, as release only occurs in finalize_recovery, limiting concurrent recovery efforts.  

## Assessing Potential Workarounds  
- Initial thoughts suggest sharing vehicles or skipping vehicle assignment for some components, but all finalize actions mandate vehicle_assigned.  
- No alternate actions exist to achieve component_recovery_complete without vehicle assignment, highlighting the need for a solution within vehicle limits.

## Examining Segment Recovery  
- Segment recovery involves actions like sign_operator_and_mark_origin, requiring schedule_confirmed, operator_assigned, and in_zone, setting ready and validated.  
- Process_origin_material_deployment, if material used, also needs schedule_confirmed and time_assigned, setting flagged and validated, but requires material_assigned_origin.  

- Assign_restoration_to_origin follows, needing ready, validated, selected, and time_confirmed, then sets restoration_scheduled.  
- Both origin and destination segments must be ready and validated to instantiate_contingency_plan, suggesting sequential preparation.  
- Attaching claims to segments requires restoration_scheduled and time_assigned, with finalize_restoration_and_release_vehicle setting recovery_complete and releasing resources.  

## Outlining Agent Recovery  
- Agent recovery depends on plan time_confirmed, which relies on segments being ready and validated, suggesting agents follow segment preparation.  
- Agents need depot_assigned_to_plan and plan_selected for allocation, with activation actions like finalize_agent_activation setting restoration_scheduled.

## Preparing Segments for Recovery  
- Flagging zones and assigning time windows to origin and destination segments prepares them for recovery, with optional release steps to free resources.  
- Operator teams are assigned and later released after marking segments, ensuring both origin and destination are ready and validated.  

## Instantiating and Confirming Contingency Plan  
- Both segments must be ready, validated, and flagged to instantiate a contingency plan, linking zones without requiring policy or service.  
- Plan time confirmation involves assigning a time window to the origin segment, enabling subsequent restoration scheduling.  

## Scheduling Restoration for Segments  
- Restoration is assigned to both segments after the plan is time-confirmed, setting restoration_scheduled for each.  
- Claims and vehicles are attached and finalized sequentially due to a single claim, with resources like vehicles released after completion for reuse.  

## Initiating Agent Recovery  
- Agent recovery begins after segments are complete, requiring depot assignment and component schedule confirmation.  
- Service options for agents are pre-assigned, but availability limits may affect concurrent recovery efforts.

## Assigning Resources to Agents  
- Agents need equipment, operator, and authority approval assigned before activation, requiring schedule confirmation and resource availability.  
- Resources like equipment, operator, and authority are limited to one each, necessitating sequential assignment to agents for recovery.  

## Sequencing Agent Recovery Steps  
- Assign equipment, operator, and authority to an agent, then authorize, equip, and activate, setting restoration_scheduled without needing policy or service.  
- After activation, assign time and attach claims, finalizing recovery by releasing vehicles and claims, but operator remains assigned until explicitly released.  

## Managing Resource Release  
- Release equipment, authority, and operator from an agent after use to make them available for the next agent, using specific release actions.  
- Releasing resources can happen after approval and equipping, before activation, to streamline sequential recovery for multiple agents.

## Finalizing Agent Activation  
- Resources like operator, equipment, and authority are released after approval, setting them as available for reuse.  
- Finalizing activation sets the agent as activated and schedules restoration, requiring prior approval without constraints.  

## Assigning Time and Claims  
- A time window is assigned to the case, followed by attaching the claim document, ensuring sequential resource availability.  
- Finalizing restoration releases vehicles and claims, marking recovery complete and freeing resources for the next agent.  

## Reusing Resources for Multiple Agents  
- The process repeats for the second agent, using released resources like equipment and operator, ensuring no conflicts.  
- Different depots and vehicles (e.g., v1 for ra2, v3 for ra1) are used, maintaining availability and alignment with initial assignments.  

## Verifying Plan Completeness  
- Service options are pre-assigned but not selected, avoiding availability checks and ensuring no policy or constraint conflicts.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
